raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Access to addiction treatment could significantly improve in California with the approval of two bills.",
    "AB 2651 will create a license for Alcohol Drug Counselors to ensure they meet minimum education requirements and protect consumers from receiving care from underqualified counselors.",
    "Patients will be able to use their medical insurance to see a counselor due to AB 2651.",
    "A comprehensive and recognized career ladder for Substance Use Disorder professionals is necessary to support recent policy advancements.",
    "AB 2376 will streamline regulatory requirements, enabling additional healthcare facilities to accept more patients for addiction and substance use disorder treatments.",
    "Current law restricts the types of facilities that can offer care for substance use disorder patients.",
    "5.3 million Californians are struggling with a substance use disorder.",
    "Drug overdose deaths in California increased from just over 4,500 in 2015 to 11,774 reported in 2023.",
    "Only 10% of Californians with an addiction receive treatment.",
    "Deaths from overdoses among teenagers (15 to 19) have risen by 370% since 2017.",
    "Deaths from overdoses among Black and Latinx Californians have risen by 200% since 2017."
  ]
}
```
